overview: This fish appears to be a Common Bream (Abramis brama), characterized by its deep, laterally compressed body. It is known to have silvery-bronze scales and can produce a thick slime often referred to as "snot". Common Bream inhabit slow-moving rivers, lakes, and canals across Europe. They are a popular coarse fish species, commonly fished using baits like worms, maggots, or sweetcorn. BadAngling +3 4 sites How to Catch Bream - Fishing Guide | BadAngling
